content_type: Educational presentation on virtual patient simulation platform for surgical decision-making assessment
reason_not_scored: This is an educational/research presentation video describing the development and validation of a game-based virtual patient simulation platform (Stanford Surgeon Assessment Game) for assessing clinical decision-making in inguinal hernia management. It is NOT a video of an FLS task performance (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, or Extracorporeal/Intracorporeal Suture). The video contains slide presentations, instructor narration, and gameplay footage of a clinical decision-making simulation, not laparoscopic surgical skills training.

video_frames_summary.content: A Game-Based Virtual Patient Platform to Assess Surgical Decision-Making by Dana T. Lin, MD and Cara A. Liebert, MD (May 26, 2020)
Definitions of Entrustable Professional Activities (EPAs), EPA Levels of Entrustment, assessment frameworks, case content creation methodology
Virtual patient simulation interface showing patient history, vital signs, imaging, and clinical decision-making options in surgical game environment
Final score display (6/10), player path visualization, validity testing summary, platform scalability roadmap

conclusion: This video is instructional/educational material about a surgical assessment platform, not a trainee performance on an FLS task. No FLS task scoring is applicable.
